metadata.note: Minimal two-source style for offline demo: us-overview at low zoom, nyc-metro at high zoom. Tiles must be present at ./tiles/us-overview.pmtiles and ./tiles/nyc-metro.pmtiles. Source-layer schema matches the protomaps daily-build (earth/water/boundaries/roads/buildings).
metadata.render-order-note: Layer order is intentional: background → earth → water → roads → boundaries → buildings. This is the standard map order so coarse `earth-low` painting coastal water as land at low zoom is corrected by `water-low` painting white over it.
metadata.overzoom-note: us-overview EARTH overzooms past zoom 7 to fill the mid-dive when the camera passes over areas outside the nyc-metro bbox. us-overview WATER has maxzoom: 8 so it does NOT overzoom — at NYC zoom only the detailed `water-high` paints, which correctly excludes the small islands (Governor's, Liberty, Ellis, Roosevelt). Without this maxzoom cap, the coarse `water-low` overzooms and hides those islands under a single white blob.
metadata.filter-note: Water filters to Polygon geometry only (the protomaps `water` source-layer also ships line features for stream centerlines, which look like odd 'water paths' through the city). Small water kinds (fountain, swimming_pool, dock, reef, basin) are also excluded. Roads filter to main classes — the us-overview road layer is highway + major_road only (interstate network for US-view context), the nyc-metro road layer adds medium_road for the avenue grid.
